summary: Decompiled two previously stubbed Sonans item functions in src/melee/it/items/itsonans.c, added the Sonans item state table, exposed related constants/prototypes in the item header, and corrected itsonansAttributes.x4 from u32 to f32 so damage-derived scaling math could type-match the implementation. The two functions were marked near-match rather than fully matched in comments: it_802CD4FC at 99.63% and it_802CD7D4 at 99.04%.
what_changed: Changed itsonansAttributes.x4 in itCommonItems.h from u32 to f32, aligning the field with floating-point calculations in Sonans damage/hit response logic.
Added #include "it/itcoll.h" to itsonans.c, presumably needed by the newly introduced item collision/state table references.
Added ItemStateTable it_803F7C70[] with three Sonans motion entries wiring animation, physics, and collision callbacks: itSonans_UnkMotion0/1/2_{Anim,Phys,Coll}.

Implemented it_802CD4FC as a near-match function handling airborne fall-speed update, oscillation/rotation state through xDD4_itemVar.sonans.x60/x64, clamping against attrs->x18, damping by it_804DD3C8, applying HSD_JObjSetRotationZ to dynamic bone index 4 using it_804DD3D0, updating hitbox state via it_80272460, and decrementing sonans.x68 by attrs->x20.

Implemented it_802CD7D4 as a near-match damage-dealt helper that initializes or updates sonans.x60 based on attrs->x8, item->xCA0, and item->xCCC_incDamageDirection, then computes sonans.x68 as min(item->xCA0 * attrs->x4, attrs->xC), returning false.
Added definitions for it_804DD3D0 = 0.017453292f and it_804DD3C8 = -0.9 in itsonans.h.
smart_moves: The struct field type correction from u32 to f32 was important because attrs->x4 is multiplied with item->xCA0 as a floating-point value in it_802CD7D4; leaving it as u32 would obscure the intended data layout and likely hurt matching/readability.
The implementation preserves item runtime state in the existing xDD4_itemVar.sonans fields rather than inventing a new abstraction, making it compatible with the broader item variable union pattern.
The code identifies repeated absolute-value comparisons as the core behavior for choosing whether to update motion magnitude and for settling oscillation below attrs->x1C.
The state table addition connects already named UnkMotion callbacks into the runtime item-state dispatch, which is a useful step beyond only decompiling isolated functions.
The PR records match quality directly in comments, making it clear these are near-matches and where future cleanup may focus.
decomp_lessons: When item special attributes participate in float arithmetic, validate the shared attribute struct types; a single wrong u32/f32 field can block or distort matching in item functions.

For item damage response functions, check item->xCA0 and item->xCCC_incDamageDirection patterns: they may combine with special attributes to drive directional motion, hitbox strength, or temporary item variables.
Item dynamic-bone rotations may use degree-to-radian constants such as 0.017453292f and HSD_JObjSetRotationZ; preserve the exact constant form when chasing a match.
Near-match code can still document behavior usefully, but explicit percentage comments such as 99.63% and 99.04% indicate future work remains and should not be treated as final matching evidence.
State tables should be recovered alongside motion callback functions because they establish how item motions map to anim/phys/coll handlers.
naming_conventions: Existing project-style anonymous item symbols were retained: it_802CD4FC, it_802CD7D4, it_803F7C70, it_804DD3C8, and it_804DD3D0.
Sonans motion callbacks use the pattern itSonans_UnkMotionN_Anim, itSonans_UnkMotionN_Phys, and itSonans_UnkMotionN_Coll.
Special attribute struct naming follows item-specific lowercase style: itsonansAttributes.

Item local variable names use common decomp shorthand such as ip/item for Item*, attrs for special attributes, angle/max_angle for rotation-related floats, and var_f1/var_f3/temp for unresolved temporaries.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used explicit f32/f64 constant declarations for rotation conversion and damping: it_804DD3D0 = 0.017453292f and it_804DD3C8 = -0.9.
Used a goto label named accumulate in it_802CD4FC, likely to better reproduce original control flow for the near-match.
Retained explicit casts in expressions such as multiplying sonans.x60 by it_804DD3C8 and passing sonans.x68 to it_80272460 as u32, suggesting type/cast placement mattered for codegen.
Implemented manual absolute-value logic with branches rather than library fabs calls, matching typical decomp style for compiler-emitted comparisons.
Corrected a special-attribute field to f32 to match the floating-point multiply and clamp sequence in it_802CD7D4.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided excerpt.
The PR context reports one issue comment, but its body was not included, so no specific reviewer feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that fully match it_802CD4FC or it_802CD7D4 after this near-match implementation.
Check whether defining it_804DD3D0 and it_804DD3C8 in itsonans.h was later moved to a C file or extern declaration to avoid header definitions.
Search for later refinements to itsonansAttributes field names, especially x4, x8, xC, x10, x14, x18, x1C, and x20.
Compare other item damage response functions using item->xCA0 and xCCC_incDamageDirection for naming and matching patterns.
Search for it_803F7C70 versus it_803F7CA0 to clarify which Sonans state table symbol is canonical.
